text: In this video we would like to present treatment of recurrent anterior transfectaric fistula with fistula plug. A thirty-six years old lady presented at our clinic complaining of persistent secretion through anal fistula. Patient developed anal fistula in two thousand seventeen. Since then she underwent lift operation, tried permacol paste. There was relapse after each procedure.

The patient is positioned in a prone jackknife position in the operating theatre. The external opening of the anal fistula is visible, and normal saline is injected into this opening to check for the presence of an internal opening.

The surgeon probes the fistula tract, curating it for treatment. A fistula plug, soaked in normal saline, is inserted into the tract. The internal opening of the fistula is closed using a 3-0 Vicryl stitch, and the length of the fistula plug is adjusted to match the length of the fistula tract.
